item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice SCP. I, unlike many people here, actually liked the existance of other instances, and the lore posted here in the discussion truly improves it a lot.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, damn. I was really drawn in by this one. It was odd and unsettling in all the right ways, with just the right amount of open-endedness that makes it stand out…but that last addenda put it over the line into goofy territory, which immediately broke my immersion and left me unsatisfied. No-vote.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Looks good to me, too. Go for it!

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Hot Damn!

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I'd rephrase the footnote as follows:

SCP-1400 does not target blind individuals or those affected by visual anomalies such as SCP-1078's.

But yes, as far as I'm concerned, go for it!

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Authors may change at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

As I was reading this article, I noticed that it doesn't mention whether SCP-1400-A can be blind or not. I imagine that since seeing SCP-1400 is such a big part of the anomaly, the answer to that question is no. Likewise, being unable to see SCP-1400 because of another anomaly, like SCP-1078 would like wise make for a poor SCP-1400-A target. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-1400's voluntary activities consist entirely, so far as can be inferred, of observation. It selects a target, designated SCP-1400-A, which is invariably a human Caucasian male of similar build and appearance to SCP-1400. Selection may be inferred by SCP-1400's movement: upon selecting SCP-1400-A, it takes up a position within 20 m of this target and begins to watch it. Though the position of its eyes cannot be observed through its mask, the motions of its head track SCP-1400-A's movements quite closely. It follows its target’s movements regardless of line of sight: barriers up to and including 5-cm lead plating do not impede accurate tracking.

To this:

SCP-1400's voluntary activities consist entirely, so far as can be inferred, of observation. It selects a target, designated SCP-1400-A, which is invariably a human Caucasian male of similar build and appearance to SCP-1400.1 Selection may be inferred by SCP-1400's movement: upon selecting SCP-1400-A, it takes up a position within 20 m of this target and begins to watch it. Though the position of its eyes cannot be observed through its mask, the motions of its head track SCP-1400-A's movements quite closely. It follows its target’s movements regardless of line of sight: barriers up to and including 5-cm lead plating do not impede accurate tracking.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. So far, SCP-1400 has not targeted individuals who are blind, or under the effects of visual anomalies including SCP-1078.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll admit there was one point where it sort of deflated for me:

Once SCP-1400's mask was completely removed, the entity rapidly melted into a dark liquid consisting of water adulterated with large amounts of heavy metals, phosphates, and human waste.

for some reason, reading that with the idea of a naked scrawny guy in a gas mask, felt like a reference to industrial black metal culture or something (especially the "heavy metals" part which felt like a *wink*wink* and "human waste"). Reading the comments and explanation makes it perfectly sensible though. I really like how subtle the implication of all the 1400s being completely unattractive is made at the end - I know it's one of the traits of the SCP outlined for the instance contained, but that part drives it home without hammering it in.

Although I don't think it's necessary to include, I'm curious as to how the sirens transitioned to this form, especially how the effect came to be centered around the headgear (unless the original sirens had some sort of headgear too?).

+1, especially because I like the more reserved SCPs (and for having a siren effect ignore gender).

The "OH NO THERE'S MORE" part really killed it for me, but I'm gonna upvote anyway because I love the concept and how the Foundation has it wrong. It's very well written, just I hate the "omg there's more out there so scary rite" part.

E: Hey, +100! Congrats!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The tone is kind of off, it degrades from a scientific detached tone to narrative fairly fast. Other than that it has no other outstanding issues and the idea is nice, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …Second point well taken. Editing now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one was too confusing for me to visualize without re - reading it two or three times, and that always pulls me out of the story.

But mostly, the fact that none of the additional instances were described in terms of their weight EXCEPT THE WOMAN really ticked me off.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate to necropost on a comment that was made for purely comedic purposes, but this is brilliant. Joreth, you're brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
True story, my initial reaction to this article's ending paragraphs were:

I am not quite sure if I can quite understand whatever I just read and yet I am strangely entranced. Famine did this, right?

I guess I was wrong on that account.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm guessing that you're planning a Tale of some kind? Ultimately it would be up to you, but if we assume that 25% of listeners are unaffected, then the probability of all 8 people being unaffected would be (0.25)8*100% = 0.0015%. The probability that at least one of them being unaffected would be (1-(0.75)8)*100% = 89.9887%

Nationality & gender don't really matter when it comes to the effect of the voice, btw.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Speaking hypothetically, what is the probability of 8 people (from Boston, Massachusetts; Bee Cave, Texas; Unknown, France; Rottenburg, Germany; Khabarovsk Krai, Russia; Unknown, New Zealand; Ullapool, Scotland; and Unknown, Midwest USA), all men, being resistant to SCP-1400's voice?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These comments are hilarious without the context of the original article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The adjective "Asiatic" should be "Asian".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Late response here, but SCP's aren't all meant to be "scary or squicky," they can also just be fascinatingly weird. In fact, I prefer just plain weird. If they're frightening, that's a bonus, but weird comes irst.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think it's that humorous at all, just more inexplicable, which is more unsettling. I wouldn't even mind this being the "main" instance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …Dammit, Joreth. XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One shudders to think what inhuman thoughts lie behind that mask.

What dreams of chronic and sustained cruelty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Started reading. Creepy, and liked it.
Saw the 'there are more OMG' part. Killed my will to upvote.
Checked here. Siren explanation.

SHUT UP AND TAKE MY UPVOTE!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so fucking weird. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't particularly find this scary as such, but it is definitely creepy. Your walking around, busy not being scared shitless by stuff, minding your own business, and suddenly you hear some muffled vocalisations, then you turn around and HOLY SHIT THERE'S A NAKED GUY WITH A GAS MASK BEHIND YOU.
Got my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one is quite creative.
And the idea of a ghostly stalker that resembles yourself and wears nothing but some sort of mask is definitely creepy and unsettling, aside from extremely weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, we didn't discuss it, but that's exactly what I was thinking, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Since when are SCP items ever fair in their victim selections? ;)

Persons wearing the mask are subsumed into it; you're effectively dead the moment you put it on. You become an extension of its weirdness, incorporeal and inhuman. No bodily functions at all. Lifespan effectively unlimited, unless you fail and your mask comes off. That's my take, at any rate; I don't think Drew and I discussed that bit.

I'll consider reworking the wording. Some of it is, I feel, necessary to maintain the internal storyline (if my meaning is unclear, read the comments on SCP-089). The rest I'll certainly have a look at polishing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I was prepared to down vote this, but this comment makes me see the article in a whole new light! 1+!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The way that some of the details are presented seems a little off and throws the tone for me. It's all little details that don't matter much on their own but add up to an issue. For example, it should be mentioned much sooner that -A is selectively deaf to the mutterings. The next sentence after the muttering is established should establish -A's deafness.

Also, it seems like the SCP is the gas mask, and the naked dude is just the most recent victim. Related: does the mask suspend bodily functions? Do mask-wearers get dehydrated/malnourished or otherwise deteriorate due to neglecting themselves in favor of stalking -A? What is the typical life-span of a mask-wearer anyway? Under current containment, it might be quite a long time between events where -B forces -A and nets a fresh mask-wearer.

Final thought, the pool of potential candidates is much lower for the space-helmet than the gas-mask. That hardly seems fair.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Are you my mummy?"

That being said, it's the best article I've read all night. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What in the fuck…
This is weird beyond belief. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The whole article.

mfw

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know some people don't like them but it's the extra ones at the end that really creep me out. just seeing people like that brrrr +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome, thanks! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mind blowing is always a good thing!

What this illustrates to me is just why this site's dedication to creepy articles written in a strict scientific format is a strength. You've got this list of unnerving observed habits that lead to certain conclusions while completely getting it wrong. That's science, though, and that's what's great about this place, and this article in particular.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hope that's a good thing…?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoa.

It's completely the opposite of what's being reported. That blows my mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You could say that they're modern sirens, analogous to the mythical Greek beauties but corrupted and new. It's implied, not stated. Here's how Drew explained it to me, when he came up with the idea during our brainstorming sessions:

…They're sirens. But not "natural" sirens like the ones in the Greek myths; these were created and they HATE it.

Part of what they are forced to do it lure more people in to become new sirens. That's why they have to stay close to the target; by whatever process, s/he has been selected to become the next "meal" &/or siren. And if these mask-men didn't loathe their existence so much, their compelling song would rapidly entice the target into removing the mask, becoming another instance. But instead, they use what little leeway they have over their powers to deafen the target to their call. But to do so, they have to focus intently and entirely on the target (hence the staring). Over the years, they've learned that they can't fully muffle the "leakage" of their calls once someone's attention has been brought to them, so instead they try to hide.

We went on to decide that they were created by a river god, just like the sirens of Greek myth. That god's river was clear and sweet, so he created creatures of unearthly beauty. It's been a long time since then, and the river is choked with filth and sludge now… so the new sirens are so hideous that they can't even bear to show their faces, much less defile the world by existing in it all at once. That's why they're masked and mostly intangible, and why they decay into foul polluted water when the mask comes off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even despite the fact that I see SCP-1400 as the Pyro from TF2, this is great, and it just gets better as the article progresses. I think there are a few spots that need smoothed out for tone ("again, this is", "Interestingly,"), and it doesn't need to be specified as intangible at the outset since that's explained later, but this is otherwise solid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is actually pretty tame on the whatthefuckery scale for me. Blame Famine for that.

I like it, mostly for the way the conflict is centered on interactions between -A and -Bs and not on the entity itself. Not sure if I like the addendum, through. I'm fine with there being other instances of other races/genders/body features, but the gas mask seems somehow very fitting to this thing, so substituting it with other kinds of mask leaves me with mixed feelings.

Also, why is this called "Sirens"? The only sirens I know is the ones from Greek mythos, and 1400 doesn't match them much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I so wanted it's ramblings to include the phrase "Are you my mummy?" but that's just the Whovian in me.

It seems interesting…but I think the last part send it spiraling into a poor direction. The fact that there are several of them, uncontained, and wearing different wacky things, seems just offputting and not in the "oh god there's more" but in the "this was good until this point" way. I almost wanna say remove those, and this article vastly improves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Creepy-weird. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only if they could hear it. "No microphones" is right in the containment procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only thing I'm slightly confused about, wouldn't the researchers observing this become SCP-1400-B?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually have my old m50 gas mask from the military still. I could try and take a picture with it, put a flat background, and offer it up for editing. I could put something on it temporarily to blackout the lenses.
Send me a message if interested, I'm about to pass out for the night.
Oh, and since I look white, even tho I'm mixed. I'm totally okay with doing a chest up picture. Just to give the impression that I'm nude.
(please don't take it the wrong way, that i'm offering to send nudes. I just would like a picture to make it weirder, and am a packrat that keeps almost everything)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoops, misplaced modifier. Thank you. I'll go fix it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought I was the only one who thought of Naked Pyro. I like the idea, but I feel that it could be fleshed out a little more. I can't pinpoint how, but it seems a, uh, a little naked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is what confused me in the reading. SCP-1400 is not the person, it's the mask.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Once SCP-1400's mask was completely removed, it rapidly melted into a dark liquid consisting of water adulterated with large amounts of heavy metals, phosphates and human waste.

When I first read this, I thought the mask melted. After going back a few times, I'm pretty sure you meant SCP-1400. Perhaps "Once SCP-1400's mask was completely removed, the entity rapidly melted into a dark liquid consisting of water adulterated with large amounts of heavy metals, phosphates and human waste." would be a way to clear this confusion up.

I'm not sure how I feel about this one. I need to chew on it for awhile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
now that the Foundation has it contained it just turns its head to stare in the direction of the new target?

To date, almost all of the new -A's have been on-site. Apparently it prefers to pick targets that are nearby, so long as they meet its criteria.

How is the MTF supposed to identify a single person at a distance based just on the direction that the SCP is looking? If the could move the SCP they could use triangulation, but with it just standing still they can't

The direction it turns to face gives them a clue, and then they can go off in that direction to re-locate SCP-1400 after it's translocated and get a fix on who the new -A is. Again, almost all have been on-site, so it's been thankfully easy. The need for an MTF is a worst case scenario in case it translocates off-site.

So do SCP-1400-B defend the masked entity if it's attacked? What if there aren't any -B around? Has the Foundation tried giving the -A a Taser or something?

The -A doesn't want to go anywhere near the damned thing, so he's not going to attack it. And if a -B tries to touch it, it translocates away. And why would they give the -A the means to injure or kill the SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the event that SCP-1400 faces any direction other than towards the current SCP-1400-A, monitoring personnel are to note the new direction

Selection may be inferred by SCP-1400's movement: upon selecting SCP-1400-A, it takes up a position within 20 m of this target and begins to watch it.

So while "in the wild" it moves to within 20m of its target, but now that the Foundation has it contained it just turns its head to stare in the direction of the new target?

immediately and dispatch a mobile task force in that direction to identify and capture the new SCP-1400-A.

How is the MTF supposed to identify a single person at a distance based just on the direction that the SCP is looking? If the could move the SCP they could use triangulation, but with it just standing still they can't.

SCP-1400-A will resist removing the mask by all means available; instances have repeatedly been killed by groups of SCP-1400-B without removing the mask.

So do SCP-1400-B defend the masked entity if it's attacked? What if there aren't any -B around? Has the Foundation tried giving the -A a Taser or something?

However, all members of SCP-1400-B who saw its face immediately recoiled.

They didn't interview any of the -B instances afterwards about what it looked like? Do they not remember? Did they all drop dead before they could be asked?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the fuck?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uhhh… too much "Wut?" for me. No vote, but it's well written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just…

It's just…

What?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, this is so out there, but its really well written and intereting. Tickles my fancy, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, that's fair enough. I'm inclined to chalk it up to de gustibus non disputandum, but you're quite entitled to feel that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We considered it, but it's too ornate. I imagine this thing as wearing a mask that's unmodified except for the opaque lenses. Thanks, though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My thoughts:
Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is weird.

Weird but good.

Upvoted.

Also, have you considered using the picture I will now post?

http://scp-wiki.wdfiles.com/local--files/files-q441-7b-a818-86/Broken%20God%20Beast.jpg
